data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Edge Higher after Mixed Market Session
data.description:
U.S. stock futures inched higher on Thursday evening after a mixed trading session. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were up 0.03%, 0.23%, and 0.08%, respectively, at 6:14 p.m. EST on December 11.

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Earlier in the day, the Dow jumped 1.3%, hitting a new closing record, driven by strong gains in non‑tech blue chips such as Visa (V), Home Depot (HD), and UnitedHealth (UNH). Also, the S&P 500 reversed early losses to finish 0.2% higher and hit its own record close. However, the Nasdaq dipped 0.3% as Oracle's (ORCL) results weighed on AI stocks.

Markets will be watching tomorrow's September wholesale inventory report and a slate of Fed speeches, both potential drivers of investor sentiment.

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: AMC Entertainment (AMC) Looks to the New Year for a Potential Stock Rebound
data.description:
The last couple of quarters for AMC Entertainment (AMC) have not been easy for its shareholders. A box office weakened by supply constraints throughout 2025, continued balance-sheet pressure, and a stock that has lost nearly half its value over the past twelve months have all weighed on sentiment. Since last December, the stock has shed almost 50% of its value.

data.title: Tesla's (TSLA) November Sales Sink to Near Four-Year Low in U.S.
data.description:
Tesla's (TSLA) U.S. sales fell nearly 23% to 39,800 vehicles in November, down from 51,513 a year earlier and dropping to its lowest level in nearly four years, according to new estimates from Cox Automotive shared exclusively with Reuters. The fall comes despite the EV giant's push to revive demand with cheaper versions of its most popular models.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

After the Trump administration ended the $7,500 federal EV tax credit at the end of September, EV demand across the industry weakened significantly. In response, Tesla rolled out "Standard" versions of its Model Y and Model 3 in October, each priced roughly $5,000 below the previous base models.

Stephanie Valdez Streaty, Cox's director of industry insights, said to Reuters that the decline shows there is not enough demand for the Standard variants that were expected to lift sales after the tax credit expired. She added that the new Standard models are also cannibalizing sales of the higher‑priced Premium versions, particularly the Model 3.

Tesla's Efforts to Lift Demand in December

Meanwhile, Tesla has been taking several steps to boost demand heading into December. The company is leaning heavily on pricing, incentives, and inventory strategies to keep vehicles moving.

The company rolled out 0% financing on the Standard Model Y, a big incentive for a version that only started deliveries a few weeks ago. Also, both the Standard Model Y and Standard Model 3 have been listed in inventory with reduced prices, making them more attractive to buyers who lost access to the $7,500 tax credit.

Moreover, Tesla has been highlighting immediate delivery for many lower-priced models on its website, another tactic to appeal to buyers who are looking to buy a car before the year's end. These moves signal Tesla is trying to stimulate demand quickly during the holiday season.

What Is the Prediction for Tesla Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 12 Holds, and 10 Sells assigned in the last three months. At $383.54, the average Tesla price target implies a 14.18% downside risk. The stock has gained 40% over the past six months.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Stanford University Ranks IBM's Granite Model as Most Transparent
data.description:
Tech giant IBM's (IBM) Granite 3.3 AI model ranked number one in Stanford University's 2025 Foundation Model Transparency Index, with a 95% score. This was far ahead of other companies. Interestingly, Stanford evaluated 13 major AI companies on how open and clear they are about how their models are built and used. IBM was the only company to score perfectly in 10 out of 15 categories, including where the data comes from, how much computing power was used, and how the company plans to reduce risks from using the AI.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Notably, IBM led across all three main areas: how the model is trained (upstream), how it works (model), and how it's used in the real world (downstream). In the "data" category alone, IBM scored 100, while eight companies scored zero. On average, the transparency score across all companies dropped this year, but IBM actually improved. IBM said that being clear about how its models work isn't just ethical but smart business. Companies want to know what's inside the AI tools they're using, just like they'd want to know the ingredients in the food they buy.

In addition, after Granite 3.3, IBM released Granite 4.0, which is even more advanced. It's open source (free for others to inspect and use), follows international safety and security standards (ISO 42001), and is cryptographically signed to prove it hasn't been tampered with. Even the smaller versions of Granite 4.0 run well on regular home computers, according to another Stanford study. IBM says its approach is like "farm-to-table" food that's clean, traceable, and trustworthy from start to finish.

Is IBM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on IBM stock based on 10 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IBM price target of $303.71 per share implies that shares are trading near fair value.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Government Shutdown Odds Surge after Senate Rejects ACA Subsidies Extension
data.description:
The odds of the government shutting down again by January 31, 2026, surged to 48% on prediction platform Kalshi after the Senate failed to pass a Democratic bill that would extend Affordable Care Act (ACA) healthcare subsidies for three years. The odds have risen by 8% during the past 24 hours and 17% during the past week.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Extending the subsidies was a major point of contention between the two parties that led to the previous shutdown, which ended on November 12 after lasting for a record 43 days.

ACA Subsidies Extension Bill Falls Short Despite Bipartisan Support

The Senate consists of 53 Republicans and 47 Democrats, and requires 60 votes for a proposal to pass. The ACA extension proposal received 51 "yes" votes, with all Democrats and four Republicans voting in favor. Senate Republicans proposed an alternative bill that would lead to some Americans receiving federal assistance to help pay out-of-pocket healthcare costs, although the measure failed to pass as well with zero Democratic votes.

The ACA open enrollment period for plans that begin on January 1, 2026, is set to close on December 15.

data.title: Ahead of Q1 Earnings, Let's Look at Who Owns Micron Technology Stock (MU)
data.description:
Memory and storage chips company Micron Technology (MU) is scheduled to announce its results for the first quarter of Fiscal 2026 on December 17. MU stock has rallied by an impressive 208% year-to-date, driven by solid demand for the company's offerings amid the ongoing artificial intelligence (AI) wave.

Ahead of the Q1 FY26 results, Citi analyst Christopher Danely raised his price target for Micron stock to $300 from $275 and reiterated a Buy rating, calling it one of the clearest beneficiaries of accelerating AI-led demand for memory chips. Danely expects Micron to deliver a "significant beat and raise," driven by a spike in DRAM (dynamic random access memory) pricing.

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Micron to report earnings per share (EPS) of $3.91, up 118% year over year. Revenue is estimated to rise 47% to $12.80 billion.

Now, according to TipRanks' Ownership Tool, public companies and individual investors own 38.78% of Micron. They are followed by mutual funds, ETFs, other institutional investors, and insiders at 28.58%, 28.39%, 3.99%, and 0.25%, respectively.

data.title: LULU Stock: Lululemon Athletica Posts Strong Financial Results, Announces CEO Departure
data.description:
Canada's Lululemon Athletica (LULU) posted strong fiscal third-quarter financial results and announced that its current CEO will depart the company in January 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Vancouver-based Lululemon announced earnings per share (EPS) of $2.59, which beat the consensus forecast on Wall Street of $2.21. Revenue for the three months ended on Nov. 2 totaled $2.57 billion, which topped the $2.48 billion expected among analysts.

Along with the better-than-expected results, Lululemon said that Calvin McDonald will step down as both CEO and a board member on Jan. 31, 2026. He will serve as a senior advisor through March 31 of next year. The company plans to search for a new CEO and board chair Marti Morfitt will assume an expanded role of executive chair, effective immediately.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Hit by Tech Weakness as Jobless Claims Normalize
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) closed Thursday's trading session higher, while the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) ended with a loss. Both ETFs were affected by weakness in the tech and AI sectors after Oracle (ORCL) reported disappointing earnings results, with major players like Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) sustaining losses in sympathy.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Before the market open, the Department of Labor announced that initial jobless claims for the week ended December 6 surged by 44,000 to 236,000, registering the largest weekly rise since 2020 and topping the consensus estimate of 220,000. At the same time, initial claims data for the previous week, which came in at a three-year low, were likely skewed because they included the Thanksgiving holiday.

"Don't read too much into the jump in jobless claims," said Navy Federal Credit Union Chief Economist Heather Long. "Smoothing it out, this still looks like an economy averaging 215,000 to 220,000 new jobless claims a week. That's not a cause for concern." Continuing jobless claims, which track ongoing unemployment benefit recipients, were 1.838 million for the week ended November 22, below the estimate of 1.95 million.

Meanwhile, the U.S. trade deficit improved to $52.8 billion in September, marking its lowest level in over five years and below the estimate of $63.1 billion. However, the year-to-date deficit is still 17.2%, or $112.6 billion, higher than the same period in 2024. During the month, exports of non-monetary gold and consumer goods rose, while imports of computers, computer accessories, and electronics fell. Notably, the U.S. trade deficit with China fell by $4 billion to $11.4 billion, while its deficit with Switzerland during the previous month changed to a surplus of $6.6 billion. The deficit is calculated by subtracting exports from imports.

The falling deficit comes despite November's tariff revenue falling to $30.76 billion from $31.35 billion in October. President Trump has toned down his aggressive tariff stance in recent weeks and signed an executive order last month that excludes certain food and beverage items from reciprocal tariffs, including coffee, tomatoes, oranges, and beef. Trump also told Politico that he is considering additional tariff exclusions on consumer staple products.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.21% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.35%.
